understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to move any pushable object (the selected controllable piece or any pushable block) to reach or exceed a target x-coordinate. Success occurred when at least one pushable object's x-position was at or beyond the specified target_x value, and failure occurred if the step budget was exhausted before achieving this.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement in this grid-based world operated on a 1 STEP = 3 pixels basis. The player could select different controllable objects and move them in four directions (left, right, up, down). When moving into an adjacent pushable object, that object would be pushed along with the selected piece by one STEP in the same direction. Walls and boundary objects could block movement, though their exact blocking behavior required observation to fully understand. The movement hints (blocked flags) were unreliable indicators — actual success or failure required attempting the action. Each action consumed one step from the budget, making efficient pathfinding essential.
